Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should provide an evaluation interface.
Rationale: Benchmarking ensures that evaluation results are complete, comparable, and reproducible across studies [1].
Comment:
Good proposal connected to a "benchmark integration".
See Table 16 in [4]

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should be able to consume input artifacts in various data formats.
Rationale: Supporting multiple input formats increases usability and allows integration with diverse RE data sources.

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should be able to process a specific type of software artifact.
Rationale: Handling specific artifact types enables tailored NLP processing aligned with RE task requirements.
Comment:
I assume that "elicitation source" = input requirement. This might be a req for the RA rather than the system.
According to [3]: requirement specification; user-generated text; general NL documents; use cases; legal & policy; user story; domain document; other structured text; business process model; interview script; scenario; other model; code; UML diagram
According to [4]: requirement specification; regulatory/legal artifacts; issues and user feedback; models and ontologies; technical documents and manuals; specific quality requirements; user stories and agile requirements; elicitation documents; others

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should be configurable to different languages.
Rationale: Multilingual configuration allows the tool to address RE tasks across linguistic contexts.
Locator: FR "support for different languages"; GenAI "language barrier"
Comment: This is an interesting proposal that we have not considered yet: most NLP4RE tools are contrained to a single (mostly the English) language, but an RA could make the language a parameter by default.

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should be able to handle artifacts written in a specific set of languages simultaneously.
Rationale: Supporting multiple languages simultaneously enables cross-lingual RE analysis and collaboration.
Locator: FR "support for different languages"; GenAI "language barrier"

Requirement: All major intermediate steps within the processing pipeline of an NLP4RE tool should be transparent and human-understandable.
Rationale: Transparency in intermediate processing fosters interpretability, debugging, and user trust.
Locator: FR "logging, I/O save for each step"
Comment: Another very interesting proposal that ensures transparency

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should provide a black-box interface.
Rationale: A black-box interface facilitates tool reuse and integration into broader RE workflows.
Comment: Needs to be complemented with other types of user interfaces

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should comply with all applicable legal and regulatory frameworks when processing requirements data.
Rationale: Legal and regulatory compliance ensures responsible handling of requirements data in research and industry contexts.
Comment: Future work?

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should protect personal and sensitive data during storage, processing, and transfer.
Rationale: Protecting sensitive and personal data preserves privacy and builds user confidence in the tool.
Comment:
Maybe we can make this user-oriented, or stakeholder-oriented?
QM check security-related software architecture requirements

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should be maintainable and evolvable.
Rationale: Long-term maintainability avoids tool obsolescence and promotes continuous reuse and improvement [2].
Comment: I was thinking we could go beyond this and suggest directly "microservice" as components under a unified JSON schema; but I think these would be architectural requirements.

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should make its components reusable to other tools.
Rationale: Component reusability mitigates redundant development and supports cumulative research progress [1].
Comment: Stemmed after discussion from SR10

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should remain operational and stable under unexpected input or workload variations.
Rationale: Robustness to unexpected inputs or workloads ensures reliability in diverse RE environments.
Comment: This might be like way too generic. How to customize (if needed)?

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should provide human-understandable explanations for its outputs and decisions.
Rationale: Human-understandable explanations enhance transparency and trust in automated RE analyses.
Comment:
human-understandable vs. natural language ?
Idea to focus on explainability of modules/components from an input/output (connection perspective)

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should recover (autonomously) from crashes or system failures without data loss.
Rationale: Autonomous recovery from failures preserves system continuity and prevents data loss.
Comment: See the (autonomously)

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should ensure the durability and long-term availability of stored artifacts and analysis results.
Rationale: Ensuring data durability and availability enables reproducibility and sustained research use.
Comment:
Persistence of input/output of intermediate steps (black-box design).
Focus on internal robustness.
Module X with input Y produced output Z with explanations W.

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should safeguard all data and operations against unauthorized access or tampering.
Rationale: Securing data and operations prevents unauthorized manipulation and ensures integrity.

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should guarantee that identical inputs always yield identical/similar outputs when conditions are fixed.
Rationale: Reproducible outputs under identical conditions support scientific rigor and reliability.
Comment: See identical/similar, this cannot be identical under ML/DL and generative systems

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should keep track of every released version of the tool and its configuration.
Rationale: Version tracking of the tool and configurations promotes reproducibility and maintainability.
Comment: Reference NLP4RE ID Card. Extend particularly with architecture/development aspects.

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should maintain fine-grained version control of evolving requirements artifacts that it processes.
Rationale: Version control of evolving requirements artifacts supports change management and traceability.

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should meet the latency constraints required by interactive or real-time RE activities.
Rationale: Meeting domain-specific latency constraints ensures smooth user interaction during RE activities.
Comment: Here the requirement is slightly customized

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should expose confidence or probability scores with its outputs.
Rationale: Exposing confidence scores fosters interpretability and trust in system outputs.
Comment: I tried to map trustworthiness into something tangible. Can be connected with "explainable".

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should monitor and mitigate bias originating from training data or embedded models.
Rationale: Monitoring and mitigating data or model bias ensures fair and reliable RE insights.
Comment: Linked to explainability of modules, bias claims from genAI modules can be considered.

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should be capable of adapting to different application domains.
Rationale: Domain adaptability enables tool reuse across diverse RE application areas.
Comment: Similar to previous

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should handle long or complex textual contexts beyond typical model input limits.
Rationale: Handling long or complex contexts improves scalability and analytical depth.

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should provide human-understandable limitations of the input it is capable of processing.
Rationale: Declaring input limitations helps users understand tool applicability and boundaries.
Comment: Stemmed from previous

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should employ natural language processing techniques.
Rationale: Employing NLP techniques enables automated analysis and interpretation of textual artifacts.
Comment: According to [3], among most frequently used: POS Tagging; Tokenization; Parsing; Stop-word Removal; Term Extraction; Stemming; Lemmatization; Similarity Measures; TF-IDF; Sentence Splitting; Syntactic Structure; Linguistic Rules; Chunking; ML Classification; NER; SRL; ... (see Fig. 15)

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should address a particular natural language processing task
Rationale: Addressing specific NLP tasks focuses tool functionality toward targeted RE objectives.
Comment:
According to [3]: Modeling; Detection; Extraction; Classification; Tracing & Relating; Search & Retrieval
According to [4]: Elicitation; Validation; SE Tasks; Modeling; Classification; Tracing; Documentation; Defect Detection; Legal Analysis; Retrieval; Terminology Extraction
According to [5]: Elicitation; SE Tasks; Defect Detection; Modelling; Change Impact Analysis; Classification; Documentation; Retrieval; Terminology Extraction; Tracing
According to [1]: generation, detection, extraction, classification, transformation, tracing & relating, search & retrieval.

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should support or automate a requirements engineering task.
Rationale: Supporting RE tasks aligns the tool with the fundamental goals of the NLP4RE domain.
Comment:
According to [3]: Elicitation; Analysis; Modelling; Management; Validation & Verification; Testing; Design
According to [4]: Elicitation; Analysis; Modeling; Management; Validation & Verification; Other
According to [1]: elicitation, analysis, modeling, validation & verification, management, multiple, or other.

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should include a descriptive name and a comprehensive description detailing its objectives and underlying technologies.
Rationale: A clear name and comprehensive description help users understand the tool’s objectives and allow researchers to evaluate its key technologies and extensibility [1].

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should be made publicly available online to enable replication, reuse, and continuous evolution.
Rationale: Making NLP4RE tools available online enables replication, reuse, and continuous evolution, as recommended for ensuring accessibility and long-term research impact [1][2][3].
Comment: Keep track also of distinction between process & product

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should be released under an open-source license to promote accessibility and collaborative development.
Rationale: Choosing an open-source license facilitates access, use, and evolution of NLP4RE tools by explicitly granting permissions beyond intellectual property restrictions [1].

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should be thoroughly documented to support its understanding, use, and future evolution.
Rationale: Thorough documentation, including task structure and targeted RE phases, ensures that NLP4RE tools can be effectively understood, used, and evolved by others [1][2][3].

Requirement: The dependencies of an NLP4RE tool should be documented and preserved.
Rationale: Documenting and preserving dependencies prevents tool degradation due to ecosystem changes [2].
Comment: This motivates containerization.

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should be able to produce a specific type of software artifact.
Rationale: Generating specific software artifacts supports RE workflows by producing actionable outputs.
Comment: According to [4]: requirement specification; requirement analysis report; models and designs; compliance documentation and legal artifacts; user profiles; code artifacts; interview scripts and questionnaires; traceability artifacts; testing artifacts; process descriptions

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should provide traceability between input and output artifacts.
Rationale: Traceability between inputs and outputs enables analysis of artifact evolution and rationale in RE tasks.
Comment: This is another descriptor for NLP4RE tools. See table 9 in [4]

Requirement: An NLP4RE tool should be able to produce output artifacts in various data formats.
Rationale: Producing output in multiple formats increases interoperability and usability across RE tools and datasets.
